children.name: insertNewTagIntoFormattedParagraph
children.docComment:
Insert ` {new_tag}` at the end of the text of the FIRST paragraph that
carries explicit formatting (a `<w:jc .../>` justification or a
`<w:ind .../>` indent in its `<w:pPr>`). Returns the edited buffer plus
the formatting tokens captured from that paragraph so the test can assert
they survive substitution.

The insertion appends a new `<w:r><w:t xml:space="preserve"> {new_tag}</w:t></w:r>`
run just before the paragraph's closing `</w:p>`, mirroring how a WYSIWYG
editor appends typed text as its own run without disturbing existing runs
or the paragraph properties.

children.name: REPLACEMENTS
children.docComment:
Stub→placeholder replacements. Each `stub` is a contiguous run of text
that exists verbatim in a single <w:t> in the source document, so the
replacement does not split the docxtemplater delimiter across runs.

Alignment column documents which paragraph the stub lives in, so the
test can assert formatting survives substitution at that exact spot.

children.name: readAuthCandidates
children.docComment:
Extract auth candidates from either the headers or the request
body. Headers take precedence when both are present.

children.name: readHeader
children.signature: readHeader(value: string | string[] | undefined): string | undefined

children.name: verifyB24Payload
children.docComment:
Verifies the received auth payload. Currently this is a sanity
check — all three fields must be present and the domain must look
like a Bitrix24 host. If `B24_APP_SECRET` is set and the request
carries an `X-B24-Signature` header, we also validate a HMAC-SHA256
over `memberId + ':' + domain`.

children.name: isPlausibleBitrixDomain
children.docComment:
Returns true if the value looks like a Bitrix24 portal domain.
Accepts `*.bitrix24.{ru,com,de,…}`, `*.bitrix.{ru,…}`, and explicit
domains configured via `B24_ALLOWED_DOMAINS` env var (comma-separated).
Also strips an optional `https?://` scheme and trailing slash.

children.name: registerAuthMiddleware
children.docComment:
Register the auth preHandler hook on the given Fastify instance.
All `/api/*` requests (except public paths) will be gated by this
hook. Call once from `buildServer()`.

children.name: B24RequestAuth
children.docComment: Shape attached to every authenticated request.

children.name: BodyAuthShape
children.docComment:
Request bodies may carry `auth` as either a string (access token)
or an object mirroring the iframe payload. We accept both shapes.

children.name: userIdCache
children.docComment:
In-memory cache: accessToken → userId. The B24 `user.current` REST
call is the only way for us to know which portal user opened the
iframe (the SDK doesn't expose it via the auth payload). Cached for 5
minutes to avoid hitting the REST API on every request.

IMPORTANT: the key MUST be the per-user access token, NOT the
`member_id`. `member_id` identifies the *portal* and is shared by
every user on it — keying the cache by it would resolve every user to
whoever opened the app first (typically the admin), so all users
would be mis-detected as that user and see the admin UI. The access
token is unique per user (and `user.current` is called with it, so
the resolved id always belongs to the token's owner). Access tokens
are stable for ~1h; a refreshed token simply re-resolves once.

children.name: PUBLIC_PATHS
children.docComment:
Paths that do NOT require authentication. Uses startsWith matching
on the raw URL path.

children.name: invalidateRoleCache
children.docComment:
Force-invalidate the admin cache. Call this from any code path that
modifies `AppSettings.adminUserIds` so the next role check refetches.

children.name: loadCurrentRole
children.docComment:
Resolve the current role for the request. Returns 'admin' if the
user id from `request.b24Auth.userId` is in `AppSettings.adminUserIds`,
otherwise 'user'. If the request is not authenticated or AppSettings
is missing, returns 'user' (least privilege).

children.name: requireAdmin
children.docComment:
Fastify preHandler that rejects with 403 if the caller is not an
admin. The auth hook must run first (it does — `registerAuthMiddleware`
uses `preHandler` and Fastify runs them in registration order, so
the global auth hook fires before per-route preHandlers).

On success the middleware does nothing. On failure it throws via
`reply.forbidden(...)` which

children.name: CachedAdmins
children.docComment:
AppSettings is a singleton row that changes infrequently — we keep a
tiny in-memory cache (TTL 30s) to avoid hitting SQLite on every
mutation. The cache is invalidated externally by anyone updating
the row (call `invalidateRoleCache()` after a settings.update).

children.name: resolvePrismaCli
children.docComment: Resolve the absolute path to the local `prisma` CLI entry script.

children.name: ensureSchema
children.docComment:
Run `prisma db push` against the database in `DATABASE_URL`. Returns
true when the schema was applied, false when it was skipped or failed
(the caller logs and continues either way).

children.name: invalidateDealFieldsCache
children.docComment: Exported for tests + cache-busting if a UF_CRM field is created.

children.name: pickPrimaryContact
children.signature: pickPrimaryContact(list: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): Record<string, unknown> | null
children.docComment:
Picks the contact marked as IS_PRIMARY === 'Y'. Falls back to the
first contact in the list, or null if there are none.

children.name: mapB24Error
children.docComment:
Translate B24Error into the closest matching Fastify HTTP error so
the global error handler renders a sensible JSON envelope.

children.name: DEAL_FIELDS_TTL_MS
children.docComment: Five minutes — see x0t.4 design notes.

children.name: dealFieldsCache
children.docComment: Per-portal cache of `crm.deal.fields` responses.

children.name: contactFieldsCache
children.docComment: Per-portal cache of `crm.contact.fields` and `crm.company.fields`.

children.name: userFieldsCache
children.docComment: Per-portal cache of `user.fields` (responsible-user schema).

children.id: apps/backend/src/routes/fieldPresets.test.ts:{ normalizeName, normalizeValueMode, normalizeOrder, normalizeOptions, parseOptions, toPresetDto }
children.name: { normalizeName, normalizeValueMode, normalizeOrder, normalizeOptions, parseOptions, toPresetDto }
normalizeOptions
children.docComment:
Coerce an arbitrary `options` payload into a clean `SelectOption[]`:
keep only entries with a non-empty string label, trim both fields.

children.name: parseOptions
children.docComment: Parse the JSON `options` column, tolerating corrupt data.

children.name: __test
children.docComment:
Internal helpers exposed for unit tests (see `fieldPresets.test.ts`).
Not part of the public route surface.

children.name: fetchDealContext
children.docComment:
Two-step batch fetch of a deal + its primary contact + its linked
company. Mirrors `routes/deal.ts::GET /api/deal/:id/data` so the
preview pane sees the exact same shape as the production
generation pipeline. Phase 5 will replace this with a shared
`services/dealData.ts`.

children.name: BindingResult
children.docComment:
Result of the deal-binding step. Reported in the response so the
frontend can show a yellow warning when the binding step failed
but the file still uploaded successfully.

children.name: registerHealthRoute
children.docComment:
Registers the GET /health endpoint.

Used by local development and container orchestration to check that
the Fastify instance is up. Returns a minimal JSON payload with the
service status and uptime in seconds.

children.name: toAppSettings
children.docComment:
Convert a Prisma row into the public `AppSettings` shape used by
the shared types package. The DB stores `adminUserIds` as JSON.

children.name: resolveHandlerBaseUrl
children.signature: resolveHandlerBaseUrl(explicit: string | undefined, reply: FastifyReply): string | null
children.docComment:
Resolve and validate the public HTTPS handler base URL used as the
iframe src for placement handlers. Priority: explicit value (from the
request body) > FRONTEND_PUBLIC_URL > PUBLIC_URL > FRONTEND_URL.

Returns the cleaned base URL (no trailing slash) on success. On
failure it sends a 400 reply itself and returns `null`, so callers
must `return` early when the result is null. We deliberately reject
non-HTTPS URLs because Bitrix24 answers `ERROR_WRONG_HANDLER_URL`.

children.name: InstallStatusResponse
children.docComment: Returned by `GET /api/install/status`.

children.name: DEAL_PLACEMENT_CATALOG
children.docComment:
Catalog of CRM deal embedding locations the app exposes in the
Settings UI ("Места встройки"). Each entry maps a Bitrix24 placement
code to a friendly title/description and the `?view=` the iframe is
opened in. This is the single source of truth for which placements an
admin may bind via `POST /api/placements`.

IMPORTANT: a code can only be bound if it's also declared in the local
app manifest on the portal — otherwise `placement.bind` returns an
error, which we surface back to the user.

children.name: requireSettings
children.docComment:
Ensure the AppSettings row exists. Unlike `POST /api/install`,
this does not require a non-empty admin list — the install flow
should run first. If the row is missing we throw so routes return
409 Conflict instead of silently creating a half-initialized row.

children.name: normalizeUserField
children.docComment:
Coerce a Bitrix24 userfield record into our DTO. Bitrix returns the
keys in SHOUTY_CASE and some of them as numeric strings.

children.name: DealFileFieldDTO
children.docComment: Simplified UF field metadata returned to the SettingsPage.

children.name: normalizeSelectOptions
children.docComment:
Validate and normalize a `select` field's options. Drops entries with an
empty label, trims and length-caps both label and value, and caps the
total count. Returns the cleaned array (possibly empty).

children.name: parseSelectOptions
children.docComment: Parse the JSON-encoded options column back into an array (safe).

children.name: normalizeValueMode
children.docComment: Allowed `select` substitution modes; anything else falls back to "direct".

children.name: normalizeFieldsInput
children.docComment:
Validate and normalize the manual-field array sent by the client.
Drops entries with an empty fieldKey and de-duplicates by fieldKey
(last write wins). `order` is derived from array position so the
generate form reflects the order in which the admin authored them.

children.name: substituteFormulaTagsForPreview
children.signature: substituteFormulaTagsForPreview(html: string, formulas: Record<string, FormulaEvaluationResult>): string
children.docComment: Rewrite every `` node inside `html` so it carries `data-computed-value` and its inner text is the computed value (or the original label on error). The TipTap-rendered span has a stable shape — `...` — so a tolerant regex is safe and avoids pulling in cheerio. The two flags it preserves: - data-formula-key, -label, -expression → still needed by the frontend to render the FormulaTag pill in preview mode. - data-computed-value="…" → new attribute that carries the evaluated value for the frontend tooltip. - data-formula-error="…" (optional) → set if evaluation failed; the frontend renders an error badge.

children.name: escapeAttr
children.docComment: Minimal HTML attribute escaper.

children.name: escapeHtml
children.docComment: Minimal HTML text escaper (preserves spaces).

children.name: FormulaInput
children.docComment: Shape of a formula sent by the client when saving a template.

children.name: TemplateFieldInput
children.docComment: Shape of a manual field sent by the client when saving a template.

children.name: FIELD_TYPES
children.docComment: Allowed manual-field types; anything else falls back to "text".

children.name: SELECT_MAX_OPTIONS
children.docComment: Caps to keep a `select` field's option set reasonable.

children.name: FIELD_DEFAULTS
children.docComment: Allowed default-value tokens. Anything else is dropped (no default).

children.name: ThemeDTO
children.docComment:
Public DTO returned by the theme endpoints. Mirrors the Prisma row
but converts dates to ISO strings so the frontend can consume it
without a date parser.

children.name: PortalUser
children.docComment: Public shape returned to the frontend.

children.name: readDocumentIdEntry
children.signature: readDocumentIdEntry(documentId: WebhookRunBody['document_id'], index: number): string | undefined
children.docComment:
Extract `document_id[2]` regardless of whether qs parsed it as an
array (sparse numeric keys collapse to an array when they're dense
enough) or as an object keyed by string indices (Bitrix sometimes
sends `document_id[0]=..&document_id[1]=..&document_id[2]=..` with
gaps that force qs into object mode).

children.name: parseDealIdFromDocumentId
children.docComment:
Parse a Bitrix document identifier into a numeric deal id.

Supported input formats:
  - `DEAL_123`              — classic CRM pipeline (the common case)
  - `DEAL_FLEXIBLE_2_123`   — Smart Process / flexible entity. We
    ignore the entity type prefix and extract the trailing numeric
    id so the pipeline can still run (it will call crm.item.get
    internally via the regular deal context helpers — if the caller
    misuses this on a non-deal entity, the deal context step will
    return 404 and propagate as a per-template failure).

Returns `null` if the format isn't recognised.

children.name: normaliseDomain
children.docComment:
Normalise a Bitrix portal domain for comparison. The value can be
stored in AppSettings with or without the `https://` scheme (and
sometimes with a trailing slash or path), while the robot payload
always sends a bare host like `example.bitrix24.ru`. We strip both
forms down to just the lowercase host so the equality check works.

children.name: pickString
children.docComment:
Extract a trimmed string from any of the known key aliases that
Bitrix / users might send (snake_case vs camelCase).

children.name: summariseSuccess
children.docComment:
Summarise a successful generation into the per-template response
shape. Keeps the webhook response envelope small — we don't echo
the full formula map on the webhook side because the robot caller
cannot do anything useful with it.

children.name: sendBizprocAck
children.signature: sendBizprocAck(client: B24Client, eventToken: string, logger: FastifyBaseLogger): Promise<void>
children.docComment:
Fire-and-forget ack for the bizproc engine. Bitrix calls this
`bizproc.event.send` — it tells the waiting workflow step that the
external activity has completed. Absence of `event_token` means the
robot was configured without the "wait for response" flag, in which
case there's nothing to ack.

We intentionally swallow all errors from this call — the generation
itself already succeeded, and we don't want a bizproc ack failure
to turn the whole response into a 500.

children.name: sanitiseBodyForLog
children.docComment:
Strip secret-ish fields out of the parsed body for logging.
We want to see the shape of what the robot sent without leaking
tokens into the log file. Access/application tokens are truncated
to the first 6 chars so mismatches are still debuggable.

children.name: sanitiseRawBodyForLog
children.docComment:
Mask the raw urlencoded body the same way — we don't want the
unredacted access_token hitting the log, but we want enough of it
to correlate with incoming webhooks.

children.name: resolvePublicBaseUrl
children.docComment:
Resolve the public base URL used in generated webhook URLs. We mirror
the precedence used by `routes/install.ts` so the admin only has to
configure PUBLIC_URL (or FRONTEND_PUBLIC_URL) in one place.

children.name: WebhookRow
children.docComment: Prisma row shape we rely on for DTO mapping.

children.name: buildServer
children.docComment:
Build a Fastify instance with all plugins, middleware and routes
registered. Kept as a factory so integration tests can spin up
their own instance.

children.name: start
children.docComment: Process entry point. Starts the server on BACKEND_PORT (default 3001).

children.name: pickLocalizedLabel
children.docComment:
Pull a human label out of a value that may be either a plain string
(built-in fields) or a locale-keyed object (UF_CRM_* fields).
Mirrors the logic in routes/settings.ts::pickLocalized — duplicated
here so this module stays self-contained.

children.name: normalizeProductRow
children.docComment:
Coerce a raw product-row record from `crm.deal.productrows.get`
into the typed `ProductRow` shape. Numeric fields that arrive as
strings are parsed to numbers; missing keys default to 0 / ''.

children.name: normalizeDealField
children.signature: normalizeDealField(code: string, meta: RawDealField, userFieldLabel?: string): DealField

children.name: normalizeUserField
children.docComment:
Map a raw `user.userfield.list` record (a portal-user UF_USR_* field)
into the shared `DealField` shape consumed by the FieldPicker.

Unlike `crm.<entity>.fields`, this method exposes the field metadata
directly: `FIELD_NAME`, `USER_TYPE_ID` (string/enumeration/money/…),
`MULTIPLE`/`MANDATORY` flags and, for enumeration fields, a `LIST`
array of `{ ID, VALUE }` options. Human labels are not guaranteed —
we read `EDIT_FORM_LABEL`/`LIST_COLUMN_LABEL` when present and fall
back to the technical `FIELD_NAME`.

children.name: appendParam
children.signature: appendParam(query: URLSearchParams, key: string, value: unknown): void
children.docComment:
Recursively flatten a value into URLSearchParams-compatible
key/value pairs using Bitrix24's PHP-style array syntax
(`fields[NAME]=foo&fields[VALUES][0]=bar`). Used by the batch
builder.

children.name: B24Error
children.docComment:
Custom error class for Bitrix24 REST failures. Includes the
upstream error code, the description, and (when available) the
HTTP status of the response.

children.name: B24Client
children.docComment:
REST wrapper. Construct one per request — instances are cheap and
carry only the access token + portal pair.

children.name: B24ClientConfig
children.docComment: Configuration accepted by the B24Client constructor.

children.name: B24Envelope
children.docComment: Shape of the canonical Bitrix24 REST envelope.

children.name: B24BatchCall
children.docComment: A single call inside a batch request.

children.name: B24BatchResult
children.docComment:
Bitrix24 batch envelope. The batch endpoint returns one envelope
per call, keyed by the original call name.

children.name: B24DiskFile
children.docComment: Disk file metadata returned by `disk.folder.uploadfile`.

children.name: RawDealField
children.docComment:
Raw shape of an entry in `crm.deal.fields`. Built-in fields use
plain strings for the label keys, but UF_CRM_* fields come back
with localized objects keyed by language ({ ru, en, ... }), so
each label slot is typed as `unknown` and resolved by
`pickLocalizedLabel`.

children.name: envelope
children.docComment: Build a minimal fetch-like Response wrapper around an envelope.

children.name: fakeFetch
children.docComment: A fake fetch that dispatches on the method segment of the REST URL.

children.name: Entry
children.docComment:
Tiny in-memory TTL cache used by the REST proxy routes to avoid
pounding the Bitrix24 API with `crm.deal.fields` calls (which are
shape-stable but expensive).

Not a real cache library — kept dependency-free on purpose. Each
entry stores `value` and `expiresAt` (epoch ms). `get` returns
`undefined` for missing or expired keys; `set` overwrites.

children.name: batchEnvelope
children.docComment: Build a fetch-like Response around a batch envelope.

children.name: dealFetch
children.signature: dealFetch(stage1: Record<string, unknown>, stage2: Record<string, unknown>): typeof fetch

children.name: makeClient
children.signature: makeClient(stage1: Record<string, unknown>, stage2: Record<string, unknown>): B24Client

children.name: getDealContext
children.signature: getDealContext(client: B24Client, dealId: number | string, options: GetDealContextOptions = {}): Promise<FormulaContext>
children.docComment:
Fetch a deal together with its primary contact and linked company,
then return the flat `FormulaContext` shape used by the mathjs
sandbox.

The function performs at most two Bitrix24 `batch` calls:

  - Stage 1: `crm.deal.get` + `crm.deal.contact.items.get`
    (+ `crm.deal.productrows.get` when `fetchProducts` is true)
  - Stage 2 (conditional): `crm.contact.get` + `crm.company.get`
  - Stage 3 (conditional): product image download when
    `fetchProductImages` is true

Throws `DealDataError` if the deal itself is missing or the upstream
returns an error for the `deal` slot. Missing contact/company is
treated as "not attached" and returns an empty object for that key.

children.name: normalizeProductRow
children.docComment:
Coerce a raw product-row record from the batch response into the
typed `ProductRow` shape.

children.name: attachProductImages
children.docComment:
For each product row with a non-zero PRODUCT_ID, fetch the catalog
product data via `catalog.product.get` to extract image URLs
(PREVIEW_PICTURE, DETAIL_PICTURE, MORE_PHOTO), then download them
as base64. Downloads are batched with a concurrency limit.

Strategy: `catalog.product.get` returns image fields as objects with
a direct `src`/`url` that can be fetched without signed tokens
(unlike `catalog.productImage.list` download URLs which are
session-bound and expire quickly).

children.name: extractImageUrl
children.docComment:
Extract a downloadable image URL from a Bitrix24 image field value.
The field may be: an object `{ id, url, urlMachine, src }`, a plain
URL string, a numeric file ID (not downloadable), or null.

children.name: flattenEntity
children.docComment:
Flatten a raw Bitrix24 entity record so multi-value fields become
simple scalars and the result plays well with the mathjs expression
engine.

 - `PHONE: [{ VALUE: '+1' }, ...]` → `PHONE: '+1'`
 - `EMAIL`/`WEB`/`IM` follow the same rule.
 - Arrays of primitives keep their first element (`[ '1', '2' ]` → `'1'`).
 - Arrays of plain objects without `VALUE` are left as-is because
   the caller may want to reach into specific keys via mathjs.
 - Null/undefined values are skipped — the key is still present on
   the returned object so accessors don't throw.

children.name: flattenValue
children.docComment: Flatten a single field value. See `flattenEntity` for the rules.

children.name: DealDataError
children.docComment:
Thrown when the deal record cannot be loaded from Bitrix24. Kept
separate from `B24Error` so route handlers can return a clean 404
instead of a bad-gateway error.

children.name: GetDealContextOptions
children.docComment: Options that control which additional data getDealContext loads.

children.name: IMAGE_CONCURRENCY
children.docComment: Max number of concurrent image downloads to avoid overwhelming B24.

children.name: __internal
children.docComment:
Small helper that tests can use instead of spinning up a real
B24Client. Exposed so `__tests__/dealData.test.ts` (Phase 6) can
exercise the shape normalization without touching the network.

children.name: buildDocxFromHtml
children.docComment:
Convert an HTML string into a Word `.docx` Buffer.

If a `formulas` map is provided, every formula-tag span is replaced
by its computed value (or by the label on error). The output is a
Node `Buffer` ready to be uploaded via `B24Client.uploadDiskFile`.

Throws `DocxBuildError` on empty/blank input or html-to-docx failure.

children.name: expandProductTables
children.docComment:
Expand every `<table data-product-table="true">` block in the HTML.

The convention is:
 - The **last** `<tr>` inside `<tbody>` is the *template row*. It
   contains placeholder spans (`data-product-field`, `data-product-image`,
   `data-product-index`) that are substituted per product.
 - The template row is cloned N times (once per product), placeholders
   are filled, and the original template row is removed.
 - If `products` is empty the template row is simply deleted, leaving
   the table header intact but the body empty.

Tables without `data-product-table` are not touched.

children.name: fillProductRow
children.signature: fillProductRow(rowHtml: string, product: ProductRow, index: number): string
children.docComment: Substitute product placeholders inside a single template ``. Recognised placeholder patterns: - `…` → escaped field value - `…` → `` with base64 src - `…` → 1-based row number

children.name: stripFormulaTags
children.signature: stripFormulaTags(html: string, formulas: Record<string, FormulaEvaluationResult>): string
children.docComment: Replace every `…` with the evaluated value (or the original label on error). Mirrors the regex used by `routes/templates.ts::substituteFormulaTagsForPreview` but emits plain text instead of an annotated span — html-to-docx doesn't need our preview metadata.

children.name: wrapAsHtmlDocument
children.docComment: Wrap a body fragment in a minimal HTML5 document.

children.name: escapeHtmlText
children.docComment: Minimal HTML text escaper used inside text nodes.

children.name: mergeWithOriginalDocx
children.docComment:
Copy formatting assets from the original .docx into the generated
.docx. The generated document already has valid content (body with
formula values and product tables); we just upgrade its formatting
by overwriting the style/theme/font files with the originals.

Additionally, the `<w:sectPr>` (section properties) from the
original document.xml is transplanted into the generated
document.xml so page size, margins, orientation, and
header/footer references are preserved.

children.name: coerceToBuffer
children.docComment:
Normalize the html-to-docx output to a Node Buffer regardless of
which underlying type it returned. In Node, html-to-docx already
yields a Buffer; we still handle ArrayBuffer/Blob to satisfy the
type union exposed by the package.

children.name: DocxBuildError
children.docComment: Thrown when the .docx generation pipeline fails.

children.name: FORMATTING_FILES
children.docComment:
Files to copy from the original .docx into the generated .docx.
These carry the visual formatting that mammoth/html-to-docx strips.

children.name: parseDocxToHtml
children.docComment: Convert a `.docx` Buffer to HTML.

children.name: DocxParseError
children.docComment:
Thrown when mammoth fails to read the .docx file. The route layer
catches this and returns a 400 with the original message.

children.name: ParseDocxResult
children.docComment: Result returned by `parseDocxToHtml`.

children.name: defaultStyleMap
children.docComment:
Style map applied on top of mammoth's defaults. The list maps
Microsoft Word style names to HTML tags using mammoth's mini-DSL
(see https://github.com/mwilliamson/mammoth.js#writing-style-maps).

The defaults already cover Heading 1..6, Normal, lists and tables;
we add common Russian / English style names that some templates use.

children.name: buildFixtureDocx
children.docComment:
Assemble a valid in-memory .docx (OOXML ZIP) from the parts above and
return it as a Node Buffer suitable for `buildDocxFromTemplate`.

children.name: readDocumentXml
children.docComment: Read `word/document.xml` out of a rendered .docx buffer.

children.name: formula
children.docComment: Build a FormulaEvaluationResult with a successful value.

children.name: product
children.docComment: Build a minimal ProductRow with the fields the loop references.

children.name: CONTENT_TYPES_XML
children.docComment: Minimal `[Content_Types].xml` declaring the main document part.

children.name: ROOT_RELS_XML
children.docComment: Root relationships pointing at the main document part.

children.name: DOCUMENT_XML
children.docComment:
The body of `word/document.xml`. Contains:
 - a paragraph with `w:jc` (centered) and `w:ind` (indented) holding
   the {formulaTag} and {manualField} placeholders — used to verify
   both substitution and formatting preservation;
 - a product loop paragraph `{#products}{PRODUCT_NAME} — {SUM}{/products}`.

NB: each Word run (`<w:r><w:t>…</w:t></w:r>`) wraps a single contiguous
placeholder so docxtemplater can substitute it cleanly.

children.name: base64DataURLToArrayBuffer
children.docComment:
Convert a `data:image/...;base64,...` URI into an ArrayBuffer that
docxtemplater-image-module-free can embed into the archive.

children.name: createImageModule
children.docComment:
Create an ImageModule instance configured to handle base64 data URI
images. Tags prefixed with `%` in the template (e.g. `{%logo}`) are
treated as image placeholders by docxtemplater-image-module-free.

children.name: buildDataObject
children.signature: buildDataObject(formulas: Record<string, FormulaEvaluationResult>, products: ProductRow[], fieldValues: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, unknown>
children.docComment:
Build the data object that docxtemplater receives for `render()`.

- Each formula result is mapped as `data[tagKey] = evaluatedValue`.
  On error, the label is used so the document reads naturally.
- Each manual field is mapped as `data[fieldKey] = value`, using the
  same `{fieldKey}` delimiters as formulas. Formula values win on key
  collisions — manual fields only fill keys not already produced by a
  formula.
- Product rows are mapped into `data.products` — an array of plain
  objects with the fields that `{#products}` loops reference.

children.name: buildDocxFromTemplate
children.docComment:
Render a .docx template by substituting placeholders with evaluated
formula values and product data.

children.name: scanDocxPlaceholders
children.docComment:
Extract all placeholder tag names from a .docx template.

Uses docxtemplater's `InspectModule` to parse the template and
collect every tag reference. Returns a unique, sorted array of
tag names (including loop section names and their nested fields).

children.name: collectTagNames
children.signature: collectTagNames(tags: Record<string, unknown>, out: Set<string>, prefix = ''): void
children.docComment:
Recursively collect tag names from the nested object returned by
`InspectModule.getAllTags()`.

The shape is `{ tagName: {} }` for simple tags, and
`{ sectionName: { nestedTag: {} } }` for loop sections.

children.name: DocxTemplateError
children.docComment: Thrown when the .docx template processing pipeline fails.

children.name: DEFAULT_IMAGE_SIZE
children.docComment: Default image dimensions (width × height in pixels).

children.name: buildSandboxedMath
children.docComment:
Build a brand new mathjs instance and lock it down.

The official mathjs security guide
(https://mathjs.org/docs/expressions/security.html) recommends
disabling these functions to prevent code injection in user input.

children.name: formatNumber
children.docComment:
Format a numeric value according to a small set of patterns.

Supported patterns (the leading "0." just describes the typical use):
  - "0"        — integer with thousand separators
  - "0.0"      — one fractional digit
  - "0.00"     — two fractional digits (default)
  - "0.000"    — three fractional digits
  - "0%"       — integer percentage
  - "0.0%"     — one-digit percentage
  - "0.00%"    — two-digit percentage
  - "money"    — RUB-style with two fractional digits and currency
  - "usd"      — USD currency format
  - "eur"      — EUR currency format

Anything else is treated as the number of fractional digits if it
starts with "0.", otherwise the value is returned via toString().

children.name: formatDate
children.docComment:
Format a date value according to a small token set.

Supported tokens: yyyy, yy, MM, dd, HH, mm, ss
Predefined patterns: 'iso', 'date', 'datetime'.

children.name: tryParse
children.signature: tryParse(expression: string): { node: MathNode; error?: undefined } | { node?: undefined; error: string }
children.docComment: Internal: parse but never throw — return a typed result.

children.name: validateExpression
children.docComment:
Parse an expression without evaluating it. Returns ok+deps on
success or ok=false+error on failure. Used by clients to validate
a draft formula before persisting it.

children.name: findUnknownSymbol
children.docComment:
Walk the AST and return the name of the first symbol the engine
cannot resolve. Recognised symbols include: the three entity
namespaces, our custom helpers, and any function exposed on the
sandboxed math instance.

Symbols that appear immediately under an `AccessorNode` (i.e. the
"object" of `DEAL.OPPORTUNITY`) are skipped here because they are
already validated against ENTITY_SYMBOLS in collectDeps.

children.name: withMissingFieldDefaults
children.docComment:
Wrap a flat entity object (`DEAL` / `CONTACT` / `COMPANY`) in a Proxy
that returns an **empty string** for any field that is absent or
`null`, instead of `undefined`.

Why: mathjs throws "Unexpected type of argument in function
multiplyScalar (… actual: identifier | undefined …)" the moment an
`undefined` flows into an arithmetic operator. That happens whenever a
formula references a field that is not present in the scope — most
notably during the **trial validation** in
`routes/formulas.ts::POST /api/formulas/validate`, which runs the
expression against empty `{}` stub entities, but also at generation
time when a real deal simply has an empty/unset field. A perfectly
valid formula like `DEAL.OPPORTUNITY * 2` would otherwise be reported
as broken.

Defaulting to `''` makes mathjs coerce the missing value to `0` in
arithmetic (`'' * 2 → 0`) and to nothing in text concatenation, while
genuine type errors (e.g. `"abc" * 2`) still surface. The Proxy keeps
`constructor === Object` so mathjs's `isPlainObject` check — and thus
its safe property access — keeps working. Present (non-null) values
and inherited members pass through untouched; symbol keys behave
normally so iteration/`toString` are unaffected.

children.name: evaluateExpression
children.docComment:
Evaluate an expression with a typed context. The context is forwarded
verbatim to the mathjs scope; missing entity keys default to empty
objects so accessing `CONTACT.NAME` against a deal with no linked
contact yields an empty string instead of throwing.

children.name: extractDependencies
children.docComment:
Static dependency extraction. Used by the preview/generation
pipelines to decide which REST calls to make. Returns empty arrays
for an unparseable expression rather than throwing — callers should
use `validateExpression` if they need to surface errors.

children.name: toEvaluationResult
children.docComment: Coerce an arbitrary mathjs result into the public EvaluationResult shape.

children.name: ENTITY_SYMBOLS
children.docComment: Names of the three top-level entity symbols available in expressions.

children.name: PRODUCT_HELPER_NAMES
children.docComment:
Names of the product helper functions. When any of these appears as
 a call in the AST, `collectDeps` sets `products: true`.

children.name: KNOWN_NON_ENTITY_SYMBOLS
children.docComment: Allow-list of identifiers that aren't entity namespaces.

children.name: runGeneration
children.docComment:
Execute the full generation pipeline for a single (templateId, dealId)
pair. See the file header for the step list.

Throws `GenerationError` on any deterministic failure; lets genuinely
unexpected errors propagate so they surface as 500s upstream.

children.name: resolveExistingFileRefs
children.signature: resolveExistingFileRefs(client: B24Client, dealId: number, fieldName: string, logger: FastifyBaseLogger): Promise<Array<{ id: number }>>
children.docComment:
Read the ids of files currently stored in a multi-value file UF field
so they can be re-sent to `crm.item.update` and thus PRESERVED while
a new file is appended (otherwise the update would overwrite them).

Uses the universal `crm.item.get` (deal entityTypeId = 2) with
`useOriginalUfNames:Y`, as recommended by the Bitrix docs "Как
обновить и удалить файлы" — `crm.deal.get` is explicitly discouraged
for file fields and returns an inconsistent shape. With the universal
getter a multi-value file field comes back as
`[{ id, url, urlMachine }, …]`; we map each entry to `{ id }`, the
exact "keep this file" reference the updater expects.

Network/parse failures are non-fatal: we log and return an empty list,
which means the update may replace existing files rather than append —
the generation itself still succeeds.

children.name: extractFileId
children.docComment:
Pull a numeric file id out of a single multi-value file entry. Handles
the documented `{ id, url, urlMachine }` object shape plus the legacy
scalar id / `{ ID | fileId | FILE_ID }` variants some portals return.

children.name: todayRu
children.docComment: Current date as `dd.MM.yyyy` (server local time).

children.name: resolveFieldDefault
children.docComment: Resolve a field's default into a concrete string for substitution.

children.name: resolveSelectValue
children.docComment:
Turn a `select` field's chosen label into the string actually substituted
into the document. In `direct` mode the label itself is substituted; in
`mapped` mode the label is looked up in the field's options and its paired
`value` is substituted (falling back to the label if no option matches).
Non-select fields pass through unchanged.

children.name: resolveManualFieldValues
children.signature: resolveManualFieldValues(fields: FieldForResolve[], rawValues: Record<string, string> | undefined): Record<string, string>
children.docComment:
Build the final manual-field value map. A value provided by the
caller (even an empty string) is respected as-is; only when the key
is ABSENT do we fall back to the field's default. This lets the UI
(which always sends every key) override a default by clearing it,
while server-to-server callers (webhooks, which send nothing) still
get defaults like "today".

children.name: detectProductUsage
children.signature: detectProductUsage(contentHtml: string, expressions: string[]): { fetchProducts: boolean; fetchProductImages: boolean }
children.docComment:
Scan the template HTML and formula expressions to determine whether
product data (and product images) need to be fetched from Bitrix24.

children.name: BindingResult
children.docComment:
Sub-result for the optional "attach to UF_CRM_* field" step. Mirrors
the shape that `routes/generate.ts` has always returned so the
existing UI keeps rendering the yellow-warning state correctly.

children.name: FieldForResolve
children.docComment: Minimal shape of a TemplateField needed to resolve its value.

children.name: GenerationErrorKind
children.docComment:
Error kinds distinguishable by the caller. The HTTP layer maps these
to status codes; the webhook runner records them in the per-template
results array without aborting the whole call.

children.name: PRODUCT_HELPER_RE
children.docComment: Regex patterns for product-related helpers in formula expressions.

children.name: cacheImage
children.docComment: Cache a base64 data URI and return a URL path that serves it.

children.name: replaceBase64WithUrls
children.docComment:
Convert all base64 `data:image/...` src attributes in an HTML string
to cached URL paths. This makes the HTML safe to render in browsers
that block data URIs.

children.name: cleanupTimer
children.docComment: Periodic cleanup interval (runs every 5 minutes).

children.name: closePdfBrowser
children.docComment: Shut down the shared browser (call on server close).

children.name: stripFormulaTags
children.signature: stripFormulaTags(html: string, formulas: Record<string, FormulaEvaluationResult>): string

children.name: stripManualFieldTags
children.signature: stripManualFieldTags(html: string, fieldValues: Record<string, string>): string
children.docComment:
Replace every `…` manual-field placeholder with the user-provided value for K (escaped). Missing keys become an empty string so the placeholder pill never leaks into the final document. Newlines in textarea values are converted to `
` so multi-line input is preserved in the PDF.

children.name: savePortalTokens
children.docComment:
Persist a fresh set of portal OAuth tokens into AppSettings. Called
from the install route when the frontend forwards its SDK auth
payload, and from `refreshPortalTokens()` after a successful refresh.

Uses upsert so it works both during the very first install (when
AppSettings doesn't exist yet) and on subsequent reinstalls. The
`portalDomain` field is kept in sync with the forwarded `domain`.

children.name: getFreshPortalAuth
children.docComment:
Returns a guaranteed-valid access token for the installed portal.
If the stored token is still good, returns it directly. Otherwise
calls the Bitrix OAuth refresh endpoint, persists the new pair, and
returns the fresh token.

Throws `PortalAuthError` on any unrecoverable failure so callers can
map it to a clean HTTP response.

children.name: refreshPortalTokens
children.docComment:
Force a token refresh against Bitrix's OAuth endpoint. Exported so
the webhook executor can retry once if a first call returns
`expired_token` (rare — expiresAt should catch this, but clock skew
happens).

children.name: OAUTH_REFRESH_ENDPOINT
children.docComment: Refresh endpoint documented at https://apidocs.bitrix24.com/api-reference/oauth/

children.name: REFRESH_SKEW_SECONDS
children.docComment: Refresh the token when it has less than this many seconds left.

children.name: getKey
children.docComment:
Resolve the symmetric key used for token encryption. Memoized.

Prefers an explicit `TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (32 bytes encoded as base64
or hex). If absent, derives a stable 32-byte key from `B24_APP_SECRET`
via HKDF-SHA256 so the feature works out of the box on existing
installs that only have the app secret configured.

children.name: encryptToken
children.signature: encryptToken(plain: string | null | undefined): string | null
children.docComment:
Encrypt a token string for storage. Returns a self-describing string
that `decryptToken` can later parse. Empty / null input passes through
untouched so callers can forward optional fields without branching.

children.name: decryptToken
children.signature: decryptToken(stored: string | null | undefined): string | null
children.docComment:
Decrypt a token previously produced by `encryptToken`. Values that do
not carry the `enc:v1:` prefix are assumed to be legacy plaintext and
returned as-is — this lets pre-existing DB rows keep working without a
migration step; they get re-encrypted on the next write.

children.name: __resetTokenCryptoCacheForTests
children.docComment: Test-only: reset the memoized key so env changes take effect.

children.name: App
children.docComment:
Top-level application component.

The whole route tree is wrapped in `<PlacementGuard>` which:

  1. Verifies the Bitrix24 SDK is initialized (otherwise renders
     a "open me from Bitrix24" stub).
  2. Checks `/api/install/status` and redirects to `/install` if
     the application has not been installed yet.
  3. Reads the `?view=` query parameter and redirects to the
     matching React Router path. This lets us register a single
     iframe handler URL with Bitrix24 (the same URL is used for
     every placement) and dispatch on the client.

The placement-aware default landing path is decided by the guard
(CRM_DEAL_DETAIL_TAB → /generate, DEFAULT → /templates).

children.name: AdminOnly
children.signature: AdminOnly({ children, fallback = null }: AdminOnlyProps): JSX.Element

children.name: FieldList
children.signature: FieldList({ loading, error, fields, entity, onSelect }: FieldListProps)

children.name: formatType
children.docComment: Human-readable rendering of the Bitrix24 field type descriptor.

children.name: PRODUCT_FIELDS
children.docComment:
Static product row fields. Matches the backend's
`GET /api/crm/product-fields` response — hardcoded here to avoid an
extra round-trip since these fields never change.

children.name: HelperTooltipContent
children.docComment:
Содержимое всплывающей подсказки для функции — рендерится один раз
на каждую кнопку палитры. Для пилюль формул в редакторе используется
та же разметка, поэтому компонент экспортируется ниже.

children.name: FormulaBuilder
children.signature:
FormulaBuilder({
  open,
  onOpenChange,
  onInsert,
  existingKeys,
  initialValues,
  testDealId,
}: FormulaBuilderProps)

children.name: DepList
children.signature: DepList({ title, items }: { title: string; items: ReadonlyArray<string> })

children.name: HELPERS
children.docComment:
Snippet-карта функций. Описания/сигнатуры/примеры берутся из общего
справочника `HELPER_DOCS` (lib/formulaHelp.ts), здесь хранится только
шаблон вставки и смещение каретки.

children.name: PRODUCT_HELPERS
children.docComment:
Product-specific helpers — shown in a separate palette section so
admins can quickly build product-related formulas.

children.name: ManualFieldBuilder
children.signature:
ManualFieldBuilder({
  open,
  onOpenChange,
  onInsert,
  existingKeys,
  initialValues,
}: ManualFieldBuilderProps)

children.name: defaultPathForPlacement
children.docComment: Maps a Bitrix24 placement code to a default landing path.

children.name: isDealCardContext
children.docComment:
True when the app is opened inside a deal card (any CRM_DEAL_DETAIL_*
placement, or whenever a deal id is present in the placement options).
In this context the app is ALWAYS the user-facing document generator —
the admin management UI (/templates, /settings) must never appear here,
regardless of the caller's role. Those are reached from the portal's
top-menu (DEFAULT placement) instead.

children.name: portalInstallRecoveryAttempted
children.docComment:
Module-level guard so the portal-side install recovery is attempted at
most once per page load. A healthy installFinish() reloads the iframe,
which resets this on the fresh load; the flag only protects against an
installFinish that silently fails to flip Bitrix24's INSTALLED flag,
preventing a tight retry loop within a single load.

children.name: VIEW_TO_PATH
children.docComment: Maps the `?view=...` query param to a router path.

children.name: SelectOptionsEditor
children.signature:
SelectOptionsEditor({
  valueMode,
  onValueModeChange,
  options,
  onOptionsChange,
  showModeSelector = true,
}: SelectOptionsEditorProps)

children.name: TemplateUploader
children.signature:
TemplateUploader({
  themeId,
  defaultName,
  onSuccess,
  onError,
  className,
}: TemplateUploaderProps)

children.name: WebhookManagerDialog
copyToClipboard
children.docComment:
Writes `text` to the clipboard. Prefers the async Clipboard API,
falls back to a hidden `<textarea>` + `document.execCommand('copy')`
so it still works inside the Bitrix24 iframe where the async API
may be blocked by permissions policy.

children.name: RichTooltip
children.signature:
RichTooltip({
  content,
  children,
  delay = 250,
  side = 'top',
  className,
  asBlock = false,
}: RichTooltipProps)

children.name: buttonVariants
children.docComment:
shadcn/ui Button component.
Supports variants (default / destructive / outline / secondary / ghost / link)
and sizes (default / sm / lg / icon). Pass `asChild` to render
a different element while inheriting styles.

children.name: Dialog
children.docComment:
shadcn/ui Dialog component, built on Radix Dialog primitives.
Exports Root, Trigger, Content, Header, Footer, Title, Description,
and Close.

children.name: DropdownMenu
children.docComment:
shadcn/ui DropdownMenu, built on Radix primitives.
Simplified version exposing only the subset used by the app.

children.name: InputProps
children.docComment:
shadcn/ui Input component.
Forwards the ref and accepts all native <input> props.

children.name: Tabs
children.docComment: shadcn/ui Tabs component built on Radix primitives.

children.name: ToastProvider
children.docComment:
shadcn/ui Toast primitives. Used together with the `useToast` hook
and `Toaster` mount component (not included here — will be added
when centralized error handling is wired in Phase 6).

children.name: toast
children.signature: toast(input: ToastInput): { id: string; dismiss: () => void }
children.docComment: Imperative entry point — usable from outside React (e.g. fetch handlers).

children.name: useToast
children.docComment: React hook returning the current toast queue + helpers.

children.name: extractApiMessage
children.signature: extractApiMessage(payload: ApiErrorResponse | null, fallback: string): string
children.docComment: Pull the best human-readable message out of any error payload.

children.name: extractApiCode
children.docComment: Pull the stable machine code out of any error payload.

children.name: apiRequest
children.docComment: Low-level request helper. Returns parsed JSON or throws ApiError.

children.name: uploadRequest
children.signature: uploadRequest(path: string, formData: FormData, opts: UploadOptions = {}): Promise<T>

children.name: ApiError
children.docComment: Custom error thrown by the api client on non-2xx responses.

children.name: ApiErrorBody
children.docComment:
Generic JSON error envelope returned by the backend. We accept two
shapes:

  1) The Phase 6 wrapped form `{ error: { code, message, details? } }`
     produced by the central error handler in `server.ts`.
  2) The legacy flat form `{ error?, message?, statusCode? }`
     produced by fastify-sensible helpers (`reply.unauthorized(...)`).

`extractApiMessage` normalizes both into a single user-facing string.

children.name: UploadOptions
children.docComment:
Multipart upload helper. Sends `FormData` and reports progress to
the optional `onProgress` callback. Built on `XMLHttpRequest` because
`fetch` does not expose upload-progress events. The B24 auth
headers are forwarded so the backend middleware accepts the request.

children.name: PlacementCatalogItemDTO
children.docComment:
One embedding location the app offers in Settings ("куда встроить").
Returned by `GET /api/placements/catalog`. `placement` is the Bitrix24
code; `title`/`description` are the suggested defaults shown in the UI.

children.name: SelectOptionDTO
children.docComment: A single choice in a `select` manual field.

children.name: TemplatePreviewResponseDTO
children.docComment:
POST /api/templates/:id/preview — response.

The preview is a fully-substituted `.docx` (formulas + manual
`fieldValues` + product rows rendered into the admin-uploaded original
Word archive), returned base64-encoded so the frontend renders it
client-side via `docx-preview`. The legacy server-rendered `html`
field is gone.

children.name: TemplatePreviewRequestDTO
children.docComment: POST /api/templates/:id/preview — request body.

children.name: WebhookListItemDTO
children.docComment:
Webhook list item. The backend's `GET /api/webhooks` returns each
`WebhookSummary` with two extra joined fields so the UI can render a
human-readable target name without a second round-trip:

  - `themeName`    — present when `scope === 'theme'`.
  - `templateName` — present when `scope === 'template'`.

Both fields are nullable in case the referenced row was deleted.
Mutations (`create`, `patch`) return the plain `WebhookSummary`
(without the join fields), so callers should refetch the list after
a mutation to pick up a fresh `themeName`/`templateName`.

children.name: SelectValueModeDTO
children.docComment: How a `select` field turns the user's choice into the substituted value.

children.name: initB24
children.docComment:
Initialize the Bitrix24 frame SDK. Idempotent — repeated calls share
the same in-flight promise. Returns the initialized `B24Frame` so
callers may use it directly, but the recommended pattern is to call
this once at app startup and then use `getB24()` from components.

If the SDK fails to initialize (most likely because the page is not
embedded in a Bitrix24 portal), the error is captured in
`initFailed` and re-thrown — the caller is expected to render a
fallback UI based on `isB24Available()`.

children.name: getB24
children.docComment:
Returns the cached `B24Frame` instance. Throws if `initB24()` has
not been awaited yet, so the caller is forced to handle ordering.

children.name: isB24Available
children.docComment:
Returns true if the SDK initialized successfully and the app is
running inside a Bitrix24 iframe. Used by route guards to render
a "this app must be opened from Bitrix24" stub.

children.name: getB24InitError
children.docComment:
If `initB24()` failed, return the captured error. Otherwise null.
Useful for showing the underlying reason in the fallback UI.

children.name: getB24Auth
children.docComment:
Returns the current authentication payload from the SDK, or `null`
if the frame is not initialized or the token has expired.

The auth manager exposes `getAuthData()` which returns `false` when
the token has expired; we normalize that to `null` for ergonomic
`if (!auth) return ...` checks.

children.name: refreshB24Auth
children.docComment:
Forces the SDK to refresh its access token using the parent window
and returns the fresh payload. Wrap this in a try/catch — the SDK
will reject if the user has logged out of the portal.

children.name: getCurrentUserId
children.docComment:
Returns the Bitrix24 user ID for the current iframe session. The
SDK exposes user identity through the auth payload's `member_id`
(per portal) and a separate `userId` accessor on the placement
info — for our purposes we read it from `frame.auth` if available
and fall back to placement options.

children.name: getCurrentDealId
children.docComment:
Returns the deal ID extracted from the placement options, or null
if the current placement does not provide one.

The CRM_DEAL_DETAIL_TAB placement injects an `ID` option holding
the deal id; CRM_DEAL_LIST_TOOLBAR uses `entityTypeId`/selection.
For other placements this returns null.

children.name: getCurrentPlacement
children.docComment:
Returns the current placement code (e.g. "CRM_DEAL_DETAIL_TAB",
"DEFAULT") or "DEFAULT" if the SDK is not initialized.

children.name: getPlacementOptions
children.docComment:
Returns a shallow copy of the placement options object as a
Record<string, unknown>. The SDK freezes the underlying object,
so we never mutate it.

children.name: installFinishB24
children.docComment:
Signals to Bitrix24 that the installation/setup wizard has finished.

Per B24 docs, an app with a UI is considered NOT installed until
`installFinish()` is called from the install page. Until then,
placement bindings won't show up in the portal interface and
regular (non-admin) users see "ask administrator to finish install".

Calling this on the install page reloads the iframe — Bitrix24
itself takes over and re-opens the app.

children.name: isAppInstalledOnPortal
children.docComment:
Asks Bitrix24 for the REAL portal-side install status via `app.info`.

This is distinct from our backend's `GET /api/install/status`, which
only reflects whether an `AppSettings` row exists in our DB. Bitrix24
keeps its own `INSTALLED` flag that is set to `true` ONLY when
`installFinish()` has been called. The two can disagree: if a previous
install attempt saved our settings but never reached `installFinish`
(e.g. a placement.bind error aborted the flow), our DB says "installed"
while Bitrix24 keeps showing the install page on every open and regular
users see "ask administrator to finish install".

Returns:
 - `true`  — Bitrix24 considers the app fully installed.
 - `false` — Bitrix24 considers the app NOT installed (call installFinish).
 - `null`  — could not determine (SDK missing, REST error). Callers must
             treat `null` as "unknown" and not act on it destructively.

children.name: reloadParentWindow
children.docComment:
Asks Bitrix24 to refresh the deal card data after a backend mutation
(e.g. after generating a document and binding the file to a UF_CRM_*
field), so the user immediately sees the new value without F5.

Implementation: invokes `placement.call('reloadData')` — the
registered interface command for the CRM_DEAL_DETAIL_TAB placement
that re-fetches the entity. Unlike `reloadWindow()` it does NOT
reload our iframe, so the result panel of GeneratePage remains
visible.

Errors are swallowed: a failed reload is non-fatal, the user can
still refresh manually.

children.name: resizeB24WindowToContent
children.docComment:
Resizes the application iframe to match its content height so the
embedded app fills the placement area instead of showing at Bitrix24's
small default height (the "opens only halfway, empty space below"
problem).

Implementation: the B24 SDK exposes `parent.resizeWindowAuto(appNode,
minHeight, minWidth)` which measures `document.body` (or `appNode`) and
asks the portal to resize the iframe to that height — clamped to at
least `minHeight`. We pass a comfortable `minHeight` so viewport-pinned
pages (which use `h-screen` + internally scrolling columns, so their
body never overflows the iframe) still get a usable height, while
naturally-flowing pages (e.g. SettingsPage) grow to fit their content.

No-op outside the iframe; errors are swallowed (a failed resize is
cosmetic and must never break the app). The SDK sends the underlying
`resizeWindow` message with `isSafely:true`, so this never hangs.

Falls back to `parent.fitWindow()` on SDK builds without
`resizeWindowAuto`.

children.name: getB24AuthHeaders
children.docComment:
Builds the headers used by the backend's auth middleware. The
frontend sends the SDK's access_token, member_id and domain via
the X-B24-* headers; the middleware verifies them on every call.

children.name: B24FrontendAuth
children.docComment: Auth payload extracted from the iframe SDK.

children.name: extractUsedHelpers
children.docComment:
По выражению формулы возвращает список встретившихся имён функций
(только тех, что описаны в HELPER_DOCS). Используется для подсказки
над пилюлей формулы — чтобы показать справку по функциям, которые
автор использовал в expression.

children.name: HelperArg
children.docComment:
formulaHelp — справочные метаданные функций и операторов формул.

Зачем существует:
 - И FormulaBuilder (палитра функций), и редактор шаблонов
   (всплывающие подсказки над пилюлями) показывают пользователю
   одни и те же описания. Чтобы не дублировать тексты и
   сигнатуры, всё хранится в одном файле.
 - При добавлении новой функции в formulaEngine достаточно дописать
   запись сюда — UI автоматически подхватит её в подсказке.

Структура:
 - HELPER_DOCS — словарь по имени функции.
 - OPERATOR_DOCS — короткие пояснения для бинарных операторов.
 - extractUsedHelpers / extractUsedOperators — синтаксические
   помощники, которые по тексту выражения вычисляют, какие функции
   и операторы в нём встречаются (для вывода подсказок над пилюлей).

children.name: normalizeLine
children.docComment:
Нормализовать текст строки: нижний регистр, схлопнуть пробелы, обрезать
края и заменить любые числа (с разделителями) на «#», чтобы строки,
отличающиеся лишь значениями, считались одинаковыми.

children.name: buildLinePattern
children.signature: buildLinePattern(lineText: string, startOffset: number, endOffset: number): string | null
children.docComment:
Построить шаблон строки: вставить `SELECTION_MARK` на место выделения
`[startOffset, endOffset)` внутри `lineText`, затем нормализовать.
Возвращает null, если строка пустая (нечего сопоставлять).

Смещения — позиции внутри текста строки (parentOffset в ProseMirror).
Для схлопнутого выделения (startOffset === endOffset) маркер ставится в
точку курсора.

children.name: dice
children.docComment:
Коэффициент Сёренсена–Дайса по биграммам символов: 0 (нет общего) … 1
(идентичны). Устойчив к мелким различиям и перестановкам.

children.name: findFormulaSuggestion
children.signature: findFormulaSuggestion(pattern: string | null, list: ReadonlyArray<FormulaMemoryEntry>): FormulaMemoryEntry | null
children.docComment:
Найти подходящую запомненную формулу для шаблона строки `pattern`.
Сначала ищется точное совпадение шаблона; если нет — лучший нечёткий
матч с коэффициентом ≥ `SUGGEST_THRESHOLD`. Возвращает null, если ничего
подходящего нет.

children.name: addFormulaMemory
children.docComment:
Чистый редьюсер: добавить/обновить запись по `pattern` (переносится в
начало списка), дедуп по шаблону, кап `FORMULA_MEMORY_MAX`.

children.name: loadFormulaMemory
children.docComment: Прочитать историю из localStorage, терпя повреждённые/пустые данные.

children.name: saveFormulaMemory
children.docComment: Записать историю, проглатывая ошибки storage (private mode, quota).

children.name: FormulaMemoryEntry
children.docComment: Одна запомненная пара «шаблон строки → формула».

children.name: SELECTION_MARK
children.docComment: Маркер места выделения/вставки внутри строки-шаблона.

children.name: FORMULA_MEMORY_KEY
children.docComment: localStorage key для истории сопоставлений.

children.name: FORMULA_MEMORY_MAX
children.docComment: Сколько пар максимум храним.

children.name: SUGGEST_THRESHOLD
children.docComment:
Порог нечёткого совпадения (коэффициент Сёренсена–Дайса) при отсутствии
точного совпадения шаблона. Высокий, чтобы предлагать только в реально
похожих строках.

children.name: validateLocally
children.docComment:
Fast, synchronous sanity check. Catches:
 - empty / whitespace-only expressions,
 - unbalanced `(` `)`,
 - unclosed `"` / `'` string literals,
 - nothing else (mathjs does the heavy lifting server-side).

Returns a `LocalValidationResult` with `remote: false`.

children.name: validateRemote
children.docComment:
Call the backend validator. The backend parses the expression with
the sandboxed mathjs instance and reports symbol-level errors the
local validator cannot catch (unknown functions, invalid types,
etc). Always returns a `LocalValidationResult` — errors from the
HTTP layer are collapsed into `valid: false`.

children.name: generateTagKey
children.docComment:
Slugify a label into a stable, template-unique key.

The algorithm:
 1. Transliterate common Cyrillic characters so Russian labels
    become ASCII-friendly identifiers.
 2. Lowercase + replace any run of non-[a-z0-9_] with `_`.
 3. Trim leading/trailing underscores, collapse repeats.
 4. Fall back to `formula` when the result is empty.
 5. Append `_2`, `_3`, … until the key is unique within `existing`.

children.name: loadRecentFormulas
children.docComment: Read the history from localStorage, tolerating corrupt / absent data.

children.name: mergeQuickFormulas
children.docComment:
Merge the history with the default formulas so the palette is never
empty: recent first, then defaults not already present (deduped by
expression), capped at `QUICK_FORMULAS_MAX`.

children.name: addRecentFormula
children.signature: addRecentFormula(list: QuickFormula[], expression: string, label: string): QuickFormula[]
children.docComment:
Return a new history with `{ expression, label }` moved to the front,
de-duplicated by expression and capped at `QUICK_FORMULAS_MAX`. Pure.

children.name: saveRecentFormulas
children.docComment: Persist the history, swallowing storage errors (private mode, quota).

children.name: QuickFormula
children.docComment: One entry of the quick-formulas palette.

children.name: QUICK_FORMULAS_PAGE_SIZE
children.docComment: How many chips show on one palette page.

children.name: QUICK_FORMULAS_MAX
children.docComment: Max stored "recent" formulas (usage history).

children.name: QUICK_FORMULAS_STORAGE_KEY
children.docComment: localStorage key for the usage history.

children.name: DEFAULT_QUICK_FORMULAS
children.docComment:
Default set of common formulas — a seed shown until the admin has
inserted their own. Real usage promotes formulas above these.

children.name: parseBulkOptions
children.docComment:
Parse a pasted block of text into `select` options — one per non-empty
line.

When `splitValue` is true (`mapped` mode) each line is split into a label
and a value by the FIRST tab or run of 2+ spaces: the part before is the
label (shown to the user), the part after is the value (substituted into
the document). A line with no such separator becomes a label-only option.

When `splitValue` is false (`direct` mode) the whole line is the option —
no splitting, so values that contain double spaces stay intact.

children.name: mergeParsedOptions
children.docComment:
Append freshly-parsed options to an existing list, dropping empty
placeholder rows and de-duplicating by trimmed label. Pure — returns a
new array. Shared by the bulk-paste action.

children.name: isReservedTag
children.docComment:
Returns true when `tag` is a reserved product-loop / image tag that the
generation engine fills automatically and therefore must not be treated
as an unbound placeholder in the editor.

children.name: computeTagStatus
children.signature: computeTagStatus(tag: string, formulaKeys: ReadonlySet<string>, fieldKeys: ReadonlySet<string>): TagBindingStatus
children.docComment:
Compute the binding status of a single tag against the current
formula/field maps. Reserved product tags short-circuit to `reserved`;
otherwise a tag is `formula` / `field` when a matching `tagKey` /
`fieldKey` exists, and `unbound` when neither does.

children.name: TagBindingStatus
children.docComment: Binding kind for a template tag.

children.name: RESERVED_PRODUCT_TAGS
children.docComment:
Tags reserved by the product-loop / image machinery. These are filled
automatically by `buildDocxFromTemplate` when it renders the
`{#products}…{/products}` loop, so they require no admin binding.

Mirrors the `products` row object built in `docxTemplateEngine.ts`
(`buildTemplateData`) plus the loop section name itself.

children.name: reportApiError
children.docComment: Imperative version usable outside React.

children.name: useApiError
children.docComment: React hook returning a stable callback bound to the global toaster.

children.name: EMBED_MIN_HEIGHT
children.docComment: Comfortable minimum height (px) for viewport-pinned placement views.

children.name: useInvalidateRole
children.docComment:
Helper hook returning a function that invalidates the cached `me`
query — call after any mutation that may have changed the admin
list (e.g. SettingsPage save).

children.name: cn
children.docComment:
Utility to merge Tailwind classes while deduplicating conflicts.
Used across all shadcn/ui components.

children.name: queryClient
children.docComment:
Application entry point.

Order of operations:
 1. Try to initialize the Bitrix24 SDK (`initializeB24Frame`). When
    the page is opened inside a portal iframe this resolves quickly
    and provides the auth payload + placement info.
 2. Mount the React tree (whether or not the SDK was available).
    Components read `isB24Available()` to decide between the real
    UI and an "open me from Bitrix24" stub.

We deliberately do not block forever waiting for the SDK — when the
app is opened in a regular browser tab, `initB24()` will reject
after a few seconds and we still want to render the fallback UI.

children.name: useDebouncedValue
children.docComment:
Tiny custom debounce — returns a value that lags `value` by `delay`
ms. Used to coalesce rapid manual-field edits into a single preview
re-request. Mirrors the helper used on the other pages.

children.name: base64ToBytes
children.docComment: Decode a base64 string into a `Uint8Array` docx-preview can read.

children.name: applyPreviewHighlights
children.docComment:
Walk the rendered preview and replace the backend's invisible sentinel pairs
with styled `<span>`s so substituted values are colour-highlighted: formula
values (auto from the deal) amber, manual-field values (user-entered) sky.

The markers are wrapped per value, so a start+end pair almost always lands in
a single text node. When a pair is split across nodes (e.g. a multi-line
value), the stray sentinel is simply dropped — it's an invisible code point,
so the text still reads correctly, just without the highlight.

children.name: formatFieldValue
children.docComment:
Convert a raw input value into the string that goes into the
document. Date inputs yield ISO `yyyy-mm-dd`; we render them as the
Russian `dd.MM.yyyy`. Everything else passes through unchanged.

children.name: todayISO
children.docComment: Today's date as the `yyyy-mm-dd` string a `<input type="date">` expects.

children.name: initialFieldValue
children.docComment:
Initial value for a field — applies its default. Date defaults are
tokens (`today` → ISO date for the `<input type="date">`); text /
textarea / number defaults are literal values the user can edit.

children.name: ManualFieldInput
children.docComment:
Single control for a manual field on the generate form. Renders the
right input type (textarea / number / date / text), shows the label
with a required marker and the placeholder hint.

children.name: DOCX_CLASS_NAME
children.docComment:
docx-preview renders the document's own CSS (page size, fonts,
numbering) into a separate "style container". We keep that container
hidden so those Word-derived rules — which are scoped by the
`className` prefix below — are present for the body container but do
not visually bleed into the rest of the app. The body container holds
the rendered page(s) and uses the same prefix.

children.name: PREVIEW_HL_STYLES
children.docComment:
Colours for the preview-only value highlights. Formula values (auto-resolved
from the deal) get an amber wash; manual-field values (user-entered) get a
sky wash. Applied by `applyPreviewHighlights` after the docx renders. These
exist ONLY in the preview — the downloadable `.docx` has no markers.

children.name: base64ToBytes
children.docComment: Decode a base64 string into a `Uint8Array`.

children.name: TagHoverContent
children.docComment:
Body of the hover tooltip shown over a `{tag}` pill in the editor. Mirrors
the old TipTap formula tooltip: shows the tag name and what it resolves to —
a formula (label + expression), a manual field (label + type), a reserved
product-loop tag, or an unbound warning.

children.name: TagRow
children.docComment:
A single row in the "Теги шаблона" panel. Shows the tag name, its
binding status badge and the relevant actions (bind/edit a formula,
declare/edit a manual field, or remove an existing binding). Unbound
non-reserved tags are highlighted with an amber border so the admin
notices them; reserved product-loop tags are shown muted with no
actions because the engine fills them automatically.

children.name: StatusBadge
children.docComment: Small coloured badge describing a tag's binding status.

children.name: formulaDtoToInput
children.docComment: Translate a FormulaDTO into the FormulaInputDTO sent back on save.

children.name: fieldDtoToInput
children.docComment: Translate a TemplateFieldDTO into the input shape sent on save.

children.name: EDITOR_CLASS_NAME
children.docComment:
The DocxEditor ships its own (Word-like) styles via `styles.css`. We mount
the editor inside a container carrying this class and a CSS `isolation`
context so its layout/paint stays scoped to the left pane and does not
bleed into the surrounding app chrome or the right-hand tags panel.

children.name: TAG_HIGHLIGHT_STYLES
children.docComment:
Pill-style highlight for the `{tag}` placeholders the template plugin
decorates with `.docx-template-tag`. The plugin's own `TEMPLATE_DECORATION_STYLES`
only sets cursor/hover-filter, so we add the amber background that mirrors the
old TipTap formula pills. Scoped under the editor host so it can't leak. The
cursor is `help` to hint the hover tooltip.

children.name: useDebouncedValue
children.docComment:
Tiny custom debounce — we deliberately avoid pulling in `lodash` /
`use-debounce` for one input. The hook returns a value that lags
`value` by `delay` ms.

children.name: config
children.docComment:
Tailwind configuration for the b24-doc-gen frontend.
Uses the shadcn/ui HSL-based color scheme backed by CSS variables
declared in src/index.css.

children.name: PREVIEW_HIGHLIGHT_MARKERS
children.docComment:
Private-use Unicode sentinels the **preview** endpoint wraps substituted
values in, so the GeneratePage preview can colour-highlight where data came
from (formula = auto from the deal, manual field = user-entered). They are
invisible code points, survive the docx-preview render as plain text, and the
client strips them while wrapping the inner text in a styled `<span>`.

IMPORTANT: only the preview path uses these — the downloadable/generated
`.docx` is built without markers, so the final document has no highlighting.
Single source of truth shared by `routes/templates.ts` (wrap) and
`GeneratePage.tsx` (strip + highlight).

children.name: AppSettings
children.docComment:
Application-wide settings stored in the local SQLite DB.
There is always a single row (id = 1).

children.name: Theme
children.docComment:
A group (theme) of templates. Used to organize templates on the UI
and let admins categorize them. Order is a sort key (asc).

children.name: Template
children.docComment:
A document template uploaded by an admin. The original .docx file
is kept as bytes on the backend for re-editing; the HTML form
(with <formula-tag> inline nodes) is what is rendered in TipTap.

children.name: SelectOption
children.docComment:
A single choice in a `select` manual field.
- `label` is shown in the generate-form dropdown and is the option's
  stable identity (what the frontend stores/sends as the field value).
- `value` is the "real" value substituted into the document when the
  field's {@link TemplateField.valueMode} is `mapped`; ignored in `direct`.

children.name: FieldPreset
children.docComment:
A reusable `select` field preset defined by an admin in Settings.

Lets admins define a dropdown's options (and value mapping) once and
reuse it across templates: when binding a `select` manual field in the
ManualFieldBuilder they pick a preset instead of re-entering the list.
The preset only seeds the field's `options`/`valueMode` — the field
keeps its own `label`/`fieldKey`/`required`.

children.name: TemplateField
children.docComment:
A manual field attached to a template. Unlike a {@link Formula} (whose
value is computed from CRM data), a manual field is filled in by the
user at generation time. It is referenced from the template HTML by
its `fieldKey` (a `<span data-field-key="…">` placeholder pill).

children.name: Formula
children.docComment:
A formula attached to a template. It is referenced from inside
the template HTML by its `tagKey`.

Expression language: mathjs-compatible with a small number of
helpers registered on the server side (if/concat/format/upper/lower/
dateFormat). Identifiers such as DEAL.OPPORTUNITY, CONTACT.NAME,
COMPANY.TITLE, COMPANY.UF_CRM_INN refer to values injected by
the backend from Bitrix24 REST responses.

children.name: FormulaDependencies
children.docComment:
Fields a formula reads from. Used to decide which REST calls
to make when computing a preview.

children.name: DealField
children.docComment:
Simplified field descriptor matching the shape returned by
`crm.deal.fields`. Used by FieldPicker to let the admin pick
a field while building a formula.

children.name: ProductRow
children.docComment:
A single product row attached to a CRM deal. Mirrors the shape
returned by `crm.deal.productrows.get` with two optional fields
(`IMAGE_BASE64`, `IMAGE_URL`) that are populated only when the
caller requests product images via `fetchProductImages`.

children.name: FormulaContext
children.docComment:
The runtime context passed to a formula evaluator.
All three entities may be partially populated depending on what
the deal linked to at the moment of generation.

children.name: FormulaEvaluationResult
children.docComment:
Result of evaluating a single formula. Sent back to the frontend
so the preview can display both the value and the original
expression in a tooltip.

children.name: B24AuthPayload
children.docComment:
Payload extracted from a verified Bitrix24 auth token.
The backend auth middleware populates this object on the request.

children.name: InstallRequest
children.docComment: POST /api/install — request body.

children.name: InstallResponse
children.docComment: POST /api/install — response.

children.name: TemplatePreviewRequest
children.docComment:
POST /api/templates/:id/preview — request body.

The preview endpoint substitutes formula values, manual `fieldValues`
and product rows directly into the admin-uploaded original `.docx`
(via `buildDocxFromTemplate`), so it needs the same inputs as
generation: a deal to pull CRM data from and the user's manual field
values.

children.name: TemplatePreviewResponse
children.docComment:
POST /api/templates/:id/preview — response body.

The preview is a fully-substituted `.docx` (formulas + manual
`fieldValues` + product rows rendered into the original Word archive
via `buildDocxFromTemplate`), returned base64-encoded so the frontend
can render it client-side (e.g. via `docx-preview`). The legacy
server-rendered HTML preview is gone.

children.name: GenerateRequest
children.docComment: POST /api/generate — request body.

children.name: GenerateResponse
children.docComment:
POST /api/generate — response body.

The generated document is a `.docx` file: the pipeline renders the
admin-uploaded original `.docx` template via `buildDocxFromTemplate`
(substituting formula values, manual `fieldValues`, and product rows
directly into the Word archive), so the uploaded Bitrix24 Disk file and
its download URL point to a `.docx` (the legacy HTML→PDF path is gone).

children.name: ApiErrorBody
children.docComment:
Generic error envelope returned by the backend.

Phase 6 introduced the centralized error handler which always wraps
errors in `{ error: { code, message, details? } }`. The legacy flat
shape (`{ error, message }`) is still produced by some plugins, so
the frontend client tolerates both — see `apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts`.

children.name: ApiErrorEnvelope
children.docComment: Top-level envelope wrapping an `ApiErrorBody`.

children.name: ApiError
children.docComment:
Legacy / fastify-sensible flat shape. Kept for compatibility with
older clients and the plugins that bypass the central error handler.

children.name: WebhookSummary
children.docComment:
A webhook trigger that admins can attach to a Theme or a single
Template. When Bitrix24 "Outgoing webhook" robot POSTs to the
backend URL, the same generation pipeline used by the manual UI
button runs for every Template in scope.

Dates are serialized as ISO strings (not `Date`) so the shape is
safe to transport over JSON between the backend and the frontend.

children.name: TemplateFieldType
children.docComment:
Input type of a manual field. Decides which control the generate
form renders and what client-side validation applies.

`select` renders a dropdown of admin-defined {@link SelectOption}s. The
value substituted into the document depends on {@link TemplateField.valueMode}:
`direct` substitutes the chosen label, `mapped` substitutes the option's
paired `value`.

children.name: SelectValueMode
children.docComment:
How a `select` field turns the user's choice into the substituted string.
- `direct` — substitute the chosen option's label as-is.
- `mapped`  — substitute the chosen option's paired `value` (the "real"
  value), letting the admin show a friendly label but inject a code.

children.name: EntityValues
children.docComment:
Raw entity values injected by the server before formula evaluation.
Keys are the Bitrix24 field codes, values are whatever the REST
API returned (numbers, strings, arrays, etc.).

children.name: AppRole
children.docComment:
Role of the current user inside the app. Derived from
AppSettings.adminUserIds.
